sanskrit: वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः । इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥
hindiTranslation: मैं वेदोंमें सामवेद हूँ, देवताओंमें इन्द्र हूँ, इन्द्रियोंमें मन हूँ और प्राणियोंकी चेतना हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Among the Vedas, I am the Sama Veda; among the radiant beings, I am Indra. Among the senses, I am the mind; among living beings, I am consciousness.
essence: The divine is not elsewhere; it is the awareness within everything.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he appears as the highest sacred chant, as Indra among radiant beings, as the mind among the senses, and as awareness in all living things.
simpleInsight: The world is not separate from the divine; even your own mind is a meeting place with it.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna has asked for a way to see Krishna everywhere. Krishna answers by naming where his presence shines most clearly: in sacred sound, in the power of Indra, in the mind that guides the senses, and in the awareness inside every living being.
modernRelevance: You are sitting in a noisy train, trying to steady yourself before a painful conversation. The calm, the clarity, the awareness watching your fear — all of it can be seen as sacred presence, not random accident.
translationLiteral: वेदानाम् (among the Vedas) / सामवेदः (the Sama Veda) / अस्मि (I am) / देवानाम् (among the radiant beings) / अस्मि (I am) / वासवः (Indra) / इन्द्रियाणाम् (among the senses) / मनः (mind) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / भूतानाम् (among living beings) / अस्मि (I am) / चेतना (consciousness)
insight: It shifts attention from isolated objects to the living presence that animates them.

quoteVariants.text: I am the harmony in sacred sound, the mind in your senses, the awareness in all beings.
I am sound, mind, and living awareness.
The mind in your senses is already me.
See me in mind, sound, awareness.
